content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All four amendments are verified bugs. The async sleep blocking issue is confirmed at engine.rs:5553 in an async function. The unwrap semantic change is confirmed — new function unconditionally strips {"data": ...} without the __rb_wrapped sentinel, with fallback to the old function creating a two-tier system that silently unwraps legitimate payloads. The quorum formula min.saturating_sub(exhausted).max(1) is confirmed across 7 call sites with no safeguard against unnecessarily lowering thresholds when healthy members can still satisfy the original minimum. The exit-code-0 gap is confirmed — finish() never checks is_error, so exhaustion signals on successful exits are completely ignored.

## Votes

- `fr-13-97b9c613`: Accept
  Verified: fail_run is declared async at line 5494 but uses std::thread::sleep at line 5553. This blocks the tokio worker thread during the 100ms delay. While it's a failure-only retry path, blocking an async executor is a correctness defect — other tasks on the same worker thread will stall. The fix is trivial: replace with tokio::time::sleep().await, requiring no signature changes since the function is already async.
- `fr-13-6e433448`: Accept
  Verified: The new unwrap_claude_structured_output_transport_payload (lines 1706-1715) strips any single-key {"data": ...} object unconditionally, while the old function (lines 1690-1704) required both "data" and "__rb_wrapped": true (map.len() == 2 with sentinel validation). The new function falls back to the old one for non-matching payloads, but any contract that legitimately returns {"data": <value>} as its actual payload will be silently unwrapped before the sentinel check even runs. Tests confirm this behavioral difference (line 2822 vs 2846). This is a real semantic change that could corrupt contract payloads and should at minimum be audited against all contract schemas.
- `fr-13-4506e8bb`: Accept
  Verified across 7 call sites in engine.rs, completion.rs, and final_review.rs. The formula min.saturating_sub(exhausted_count).max(1) blindly reduces the quorum for every exhausted member. For a panel of 3 with min=2, exhausting the optional 3rd member drops effective_min to 1, allowing a single member to decide the stage even though 2 healthy members remain and can satisfy the original minimum. The correct approach is effective_min = max(1, min(original_min, available_members)) — only reduce when the original minimum is mathematically unsatisfiable. The code comments acknowledge this is intentional degradation, but the degradation triggers too aggressively when slack members are exhausted.
- `fr-13-3ac79d5b`: Accept
  Verified: The exhaustion classifier at line 1362/1418 is gated by !status.success(), so exit code 0 takes the fallback arm at line 1497 which calls finish() directly. finish() (lines 133+) never calls extract_stdout_error() or checks the is_error field. extract_stdout_error (lines 1614-1637) knows how to detect {"is_error":true,...} envelopes but is only reachable in the non-zero exit branch. A backend returning an is_error envelope with exit 0 will have its exhaustion signal silently ignored and the payload processed as a successful result, creating a genuine gap in the graceful degradation feature.
